global_constraints: Do not edit files outside declared file_targets unless task is blocked and replanned
No scope expansion without explicit replan approval
Keep SKILL.md compact and retrieval-oriented for AI agents
Do not replace realistic domain examples with toy entity examples

items.title: Define skill artifact structure and section map
items.objective: Lock the concrete file layout, section ordering, and retrieval boundaries for the OOP planning skill.
scope_in: Define section ordering and cross-reference boundaries

steps.instruction: Inspect current repo contents and finalize the file layout for the skill and support material.
Create the skeleton files with section placeholders and a clear boundary between SKILL.md and the support file.
Review the section map against the retrieval goals and acceptance criteria.
Commit the logical task once the structure is locked.

commands.run: rg --files
Create SKILL.md and oop-planning-examples.md with the planned section skeleton.
Review headings, anchors, and file split against docs/plans/2026-03-10-oop-planning-skill/plan.md.
git commit -m "feat(skill): define oop planning skill structure"

expected_results.expect: The current repo surface is confirmed before creating skill files.
SKILL.md and oop-planning-examples.md exist with the planned section map.
The split keeps SKILL.md compact and leaves heavy example content in the support file.
One commit records only the structure-related files for this logical task.

checks: Verify the structure satisfies AC1 and AC2

items.handoff_notes: Use the memory refs to keep the structure content-first and avoid assuming tooling details. The support file is currently planned as the place for heavier examples and anti-pattern material.

items.title: Author core workflow and heuristic guidance
items.objective: Write the core planning-first workflow and the main OOP heuristic sections for the skill.

steps.instruction: Write the frontmatter, overview, and compact planning workflow for AI-agent use.
Add the OOP planning fundamentals and SOLID validation sections with explicit decision heuristics.
Review the draft for planning-first balance and ensure it does not read like a beginner tutorial.
Commit the core guidance as one logical task.

commands.run: Author the compact workflow and overview in SKILL.md.
Author the heuristic sections in SKILL.md.
Review SKILL.md against the planning goals and acceptance criteria.
git commit -m "feat(skill): author core oop planning heuristics"

expected_results.expect: SKILL.md starts with a clear trigger-oriented entrypoint and workflow.
The core sections teach both OOP usage and non-OOP alternatives through explicit heuristics.
The draft remains advanced, planning-first, and retrieval-friendly.
One commit captures only the core SKILL.md guidance changes.

checks: Inspect SKILL.md for workflow and heuristic completeness
Verify balanced OOP/non-OOP guidance satisfies AC1 and AC3

items.handoff_notes: Use the memory refs to keep the content tied to the repo purpose and the planned two-file structure. Avoid slipping into human-beginner teaching tone.

items.title: Author real-world examples and anti-pattern catalog
items.objective: Provide realistic recurring-domain examples, clear anti-examples, and a dedicated anti-pattern catalog for the skill.

steps.instruction: Write the recurring-domain good examples and bad contrasts in the support file.
Add a dedicated anti-pattern catalog and cross-link it from SKILL.md.
Review the examples to ensure they stay non-trivial, balanced, and directly retrievable.
Commit the examples and anti-pattern guidance as one logical task.

commands.run: Author realistic recurring-domain examples in oop-planning-examples.md.
Author and link the anti-pattern catalog from SKILL.md.
Review SKILL.md and oop-planning-examples.md against AC3-AC5.
git commit -m "feat(skill): add oop examples and anti-pattern catalog"

expected_results.expect: All three recurring domains appear in realistic examples with clear design pressure.
The anti-pattern catalog is directly discoverable and clearly linked from the main skill.
Examples avoid toy entities and explicitly show when not to use classes or OOP.
One commit records the example and anti-pattern changes only.

checks: Inspect examples for all three domains
Verify anti-pattern catalog and non-OOP guidance satisfy AC3-AC5

items.handoff_notes: The key risk is drifting into fake-OOP or toy examples. Use PIT-002 to keep SOLID subordinate to the broader planning guidance.

items.title: Prepare skill verification scenarios
items.objective: Document the pressure scenarios and expected behavior changes needed to verify the skill under writing-skills rules.
scope_in: Baseline failure scenarios without the skill
Expected success scenarios with the skill

steps.instruction: Define baseline failure scenarios that likely cause agents to produce fake-OOP or class-heavy guidance without this skill.
Write RED/GREEN verification scenarios in oop-planning-examples.md and link to them from SKILL.md.
Review the verification notes against writing-skills requirements and ensure the skill references them clearly.
Commit the verification preparation as one logical task.

commands.run: Design pressure scenarios for skill verification.
Author RED/GREEN verification scenarios in oop-planning-examples.md and update SKILL.md references.
Review verification notes against writing-skills guidance.
git commit -m "test(skill): prepare oop skill verification scenarios"

expected_results.expect: Baseline scenarios target real fake-OOP failure modes, not generic documentation checks.
oop-planning-examples.md documents RED/GREEN expectations and rationalization targets, and SKILL.md routes to them.
The verification plan is clearly aligned with writing-skills and supports AC2 and AC6.
One commit captures only the verification preparation files.

checks: Inspect oop-planning-examples.md for RED/GREEN scenario coverage
Verify verification preparation satisfies AC2 and AC6

items.handoff_notes: Memory: no applicable memory ids because this task's verification scenario details are implementation-specific rather than durable repo memory. The task must still enforce the writing-skills RED/GREEN/REFACTOR expectation inside oop-planning-examples.md.

items.title: Add retrieval anchors and navigation index
items.objective: Improve support-file retrieval precision by adding explicit anchors, a compact index, and section-level routing from the main skill file.
scope_in: Add explicit HTML anchors to recurring domain sections
Add explicit HTML anchors to anti-pattern entries and verification sections
Add a compact navigation index to oop-planning-examples.md
Update SKILL.md routing text to reference named support-file sections
scope_out: Expanding RED/GREEN scenarios beyond navigation needs

steps.instruction: Add explicit HTML anchors and a compact navigation index to oop-planning-examples.md.
Update SKILL.md so routing text points to the named sections in oop-planning-examples.md.
Review navigation and retrieval precision against the implementation-review findings.
Commit the retrieval-navigation improvements as one logical task.

commands.run: Add anchors and a compact section index to oop-planning-examples.md.
Update SKILL.md routing text to point to named support-file sections.
Review SKILL.md and oop-planning-examples.md for retrieval precision improvements.
git commit -m "docs(skill): add section anchors and navigation index"

expected_results.expect: oop-planning-examples.md exposes explicit jump targets for domains, anti-patterns, and verification scenarios.
SKILL.md routes to named sections instead of only the support file as a whole.
The retrieval/navigation gap identified in A03 and H01 is reduced.
One commit records only the retrieval-navigation improvement changes.

checks: Inspect oop-planning-examples.md for anchors and navigation index

items.handoff_notes: Use OPS-004 to keep the main skill compact while making the support file easier to retrieve from. This task supersedes no completed work; it narrows a reviewed usability gap.
